items.title: Pope Leo makes his first trip outside the Vatican
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Pope Leo made an unscheduled visit to a shrine in Gennazzano, one hour from Rome, on Saturday afternoon.

items.title: Floods kill 10 and wreak havoc in Somalian capital
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Horn of Africa nation has suffered drought in the recent past, and residents have expressed surprise at the day-long downpour.

(AP video by Mohamed Sheikh Nor)

items.title: Ukraine and its allies push for 30-day ceasefire starting Monday
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Saturday also marked the last day of a unilateral three-day ceasefire declared by Russia that Ukraine says the Kremlin’s forces have repeatedly violated.

items.title: Karachi residents welcome ceasefire deal between India and Pakistan
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The deal follows weeks of clashes, missile and drone strikes across their borders that were triggered by a gun massacre of tourists last month that India blames on Pakistan, which denies the charge. Dozens of civilians have been killed on both sides.

(AP video by Muhammad Farooq)

items.title: Kali Uchis says new album helped her grieve and heal after losing her mother
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Kali Uchis says she unknowingly wrote "Sincerely," as a remedy for her future self. After losing her mom, the album became part of her healing process.
#kaliuchis #musoc #shorts</article>

items.title: Pope Leo lays identifies AI as one of the most critical matters facing humanity as he meets cardinal
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>In his first formal audience, Leo made clear he will follow in the modernizing reforms of his predecessor, Pope Francis, to make the Catholic Church inclusive.

(AP Video shot by Helena Alves)

items.content:
<article>The ceasefire deal follows weeks of clashes, missile and drone strikes across their borders that were triggered by a gun massacre of tourists last month that India blames on Pakistan, which denies the charge. Dozens of civilians have been killed on both sides.

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Footage captured by The Associated Press showed largescale damage to buildings and dozens of people combing through flaming wreckage looking for survivors.

items.title: Vatican chimney used to signal election of pope gets dismantled as faithful express hope for unity
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The chimney of the Sistine Chapel, used to signal the election of a new pope, was dismantled on Saturday as faithul continued to flock to St. Peter's Square ahead of Pope Leo XIV's first Regina Coeli prayer.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: European leaders lay candles at war memorial in Kyiv and pose for photo
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The visit marked the first time the leaders of the four countries have traveled together to Ukraine. Along with U.S. President Donald Trump, the European leaders are pushing for Russia to agree to a 30-day ceasefire to allow for peace talks on ending the conflict.

items.title: Supporters come out for final rallies ahead of Albania's parliamentary elections on Sunday
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Prime Minister Edi Rama of the left wing Socialist Party is seeking a fourth term as Albania's prime minister. His main challenger, Sali Berisha of the conservative center-right Democratic Party, a former president and prime minister, argues that Albania still isn't ready for EU membership.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.content:
<article>Kim was presented with flowers and welcomed by officials before making a speech where he praised the development of “long-standing strategic relations” between the two countries.”

items.title: Explosions heard in India and in Indian-controlled Kashmir
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A civil administrator was killed and two others wounded in Pakistani shelling in a frontier town near the highly militarized Line of Control in Indian-controlled Kashmir, officials said on Saturday.

items.title: Victory Day parade in Russia
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Russia marks the 80th anniversary of the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II. President Vladimir Putin presided over a massive parade of tanks, missiles and troops through Red Square and welcomed over two dozen world leaders.

#russia #victoryday</article>

items.title: Pakistan's military releases video it says shows missiles 'engaging Indian targets'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Pakistan's military released video on Saturday that it says shows a Fateh One missile system 'engaging Indian targets'. In the video several missiles are fired from a mobile launcher. The AP cannot independently verify the date, location or conditions under which this video was shot. Read more: https://bit.ly/42R1fIB 

#pakistan #india #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
